briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an uninitialized CAN frame buffer (can_buf)

detaileddescription.para: When CAN_BUF_INIT is used to initialize the buffer
Then the buffer is initialized, has a null pointer to memory region, zeroed size and offsets

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an uninitialized CAN frame buffer (can_buf)

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_init() is called with a null pointer to memory region and zero size
Then the buffer is initialized, has a null pointer to memory region, zeroed size and offsets

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an uninitialized CAN frame buffer (can_buf) and some memory region for storing frames

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_init() is called with a pointer to the memory region and its size
Then the buffer is initialized, has size equal to the memory region's size minus 1, pointer to requested region and is empty

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf)

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_write() is called with a null pointer to storage and 0 frames to write
Then 0 is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an empty CAN frame buffer (can_buf)

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_write() is called with a pointer to a single CAN frame and number of frames to write equal to 1
Then 1 is returned, the frame was written to the buffer, buffer's remaining capacity got decreased by 1 and size increased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an empty CAN frame buffer (can_buf) and an array of CAN frames of size less than or equal to the buffer's capacity

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_write() is called with the array and its size
Then size of the array is returned, all requested frames were written to the buffer, buffer's remaining capacity got decreased by number of frames written and size increased by the same value

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) and an array of CAN frames of size greater than the buffer's capacity

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_write() is called with the array and its size
Then the buffer's capacity from before the call is returned, same number of frames were written to the buffer, there is no space left in the buffer

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an empty CAN frame buffer (can_buf)

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_clear() is called
Then nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with some frames stored

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_clear() is called
Then the buffer is empty and at full capacity

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with some frames stored

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_peek() is called with a null pointer to storage and a number of frames requested less than or equal to the number of frames stored
Then the number of requested frames is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with some frames stored

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_peek() is called with a null pointer to storage and a number of frames requested greater than the number of frames stored
Then the number of stored frames is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with some frames stored and a memory region of size greater than or equal to the frame buffer's size

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_peek() is called with a pointer to the memory region and its size
Then the number of stored frames is returned, the memory region contains all read frames, the buffer's state is not changed

briefdescription.para: Given LELY_NO_MALLOC enabled; a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with some frames stored

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_reserve() is called with a value greater than the buffer's remaining capacity
Then 0 is returned, nothing is changed; ERRNUM_NOMEM error number is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with some frames stored

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_reserve() is called with a value less than or equal to the buffer's remaining capacity
Then buffer capacity is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given LELY_NO_MALLOC enabled; a pointer to an empty CAN frame buffer (can_buf)

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_reserve() is called with a value greater than the buffer's remaining capacity
Then 0 is returned, nothing is changed; ERRNUM_NOMEM error number is set

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf)

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_read() is called with a null pointer to storage and zero frames requested
Then 0 is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with one frame stored and a memory region to store read frames

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_read() is called with a pointer to the memory region and its size
Then 1 is returned, there are no more frames in the buffer to be read, the memory region contains the read frame

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with some frames stored and a memory region of size greater than or equal to the frame buffer's size

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_read() is called with a pointer to the memory region and its size
Then the number of stored frames is returned, there are no more frames in the buffer to be read, the buffer is at full capacity, the memory region contains all read frames

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with some frames stored

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_read() is called with a null pointer to storage and a number of frames requested less than or equal to the number of frames stored
Then the number of requested frames is returned, those frames are removed from the buffer

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an empty CAN frame buffer (can_buf)

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_size() is called
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with some frames stored

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_size() is called
Then the number of stored frames is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with no space left

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_size() is called
Then full initial buffer capacity is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to an empty CAN frame buffer (can_buf)

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_capacity() is called
Then full buffer capacity is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with some frames stored

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_capacity() is called
Then full buffer capacity decreased by number of frames stored is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a pointer to a CAN frame buffer (can_buf) with no space left

detaileddescription.para: When can_buf_capacity() is called
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen Library Unit Test Suite.

detaileddescription.para:
2020-2021 N7 Space Sp. z o.o.
Unit Test Suite was developed under a programme of, and funded by, the European Space Agency.

You may obtain a copy of the License at http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
